do: Build RSS/automation workflows with targeted filtering upfront
Condense without losing essential technical specificity
Document iterative ideation showing how concepts evolved
Prefer JSON-driven workflows over manual scripts (LLM can update JSON directly in Cascade)
Use scripts only for external APIs (Gmail, Dropbox) or performance-critical operations
Consult user when tradeoff between friction and functionality is unclear
Log active engagement duration (start to JSON integration) in summary_log entries
Track time patterns to improve task estimation accuracy
Provide time-boxed task recommendations with 10-20% buffer
dont: Pull everything from RSS feeds before filtering (causes workflow overwhelm)
Create scripts for tasks that can be done via JSON updates (adds friction)
Build status checkers or re-extraction scripts (LLM can read/process directly)
qa_loops: Reality check grand visions against pragmatic execution constraints
Test workflow logic with concrete URL/query examples

lexicon.definition: Documentation format showing iterative exploration with phases, evolution arrows, and concept progression
examples: JSON summary tracing from curiosity through frameworks to market-ready solutions
Phase-by-phase capture of how ideas built on each other

lexicon.definition: Strategic concern when potential beta tester is building competitive product
examples: Competitor beta tester may gain product insights while sharing minimal feedback
Need to assess trust level before granting early access

lexicon.definition: User-controlled validation and accuracy mechanisms for AI-generated content
examples: Keep context accurate and current through user validation
User ownership and control over memory persistence
Clarity and portability over automated novelty features

lexicon.definition: Pedagogical principle that ideas grouped in threes are more memorable and impactful for learning
examples: Decide · Document · Deliver framework for design systems
Three-beat storytelling aligns with cognitive chunking principles
Makes abstract system principles accessible and memorable

lexicon.definition: Vocabulary variations for technical concepts to improve accessibility and understanding
examples: Composition → Assembly, Construction, Configuration, Orchestration, Integration
Provides multiple entry points for different learning styles
Reduces jargon barriers in technical education

lexicon.definition: Systematic approach to creating formatted documents from structured content using HTML+CSS or Pandoc
examples: HTML + CSS → PDF via Puppeteer (visual fidelity, leverages joju patterns)
Markdown + Pandoc → multiple formats (DOCX, PDF, PPTX with document features)
Template-based styling for consistent brand/format output

lexicon.definition: Framework for converting workspace content to distributable document formats
examples: Windsurf generates files → Dropbox sync → external conversion tools
HTML + CSS for PDFs, Pandoc for multi-format academic documents
Template library for reusable document styles and structures

lexicon.definition: Tangible object with unique identity linked to persistent digital memory entry
examples: NFC/QR/serial-enabled artifacts connecting to 8825 memory capsules
Serialized objects with corresponding digital story pages
Physical tokens that unlock digital content and continuity

lexicon.definition: Limited-run products with numbered narrative arcs for collection value
examples: Kickstarter campaigns with serialized editions
Numbered artifacts with evolving story context
Small-batch manufacturing with unique identity per unit

lexicon.definition: Multi-perspective framing model for evaluating decisions to avoid single-bias thinking
examples: Customer Lens: Does this solve something they truly care about?
Pragmatic Lens: Can we deliver this with current resources?
Growth Lens: Does this create compounding advantage?
Money Lens: Does this connect meaningfully to revenue?

lexicon.definition: Small, fast validation cycles that run until the approach clicks naturally
examples: Prototype → Show → Watch reaction → Adjust → Repeat
12-month MVP horizon through tightening loops
Optimize for reactions over feedback

lexicon.definition: Explicit value proposition template for validation testing
examples: If we help [CUSTOMER] solve [PROBLEM] with [SOLUTION]
They will pick us over [COMPETITION] because [DIFFERENTIATION]
Always explicit before building, immediately testable

lexicon.definition: Prioritize emotional response signals over polite opinions for validation
examples: Observable behavior vs compliments
Live scoring over vibes
Natural adoption signals vs survey responses

lexicon.definition: System for identifying appropriate voice/style based on context signals (author, domain, audience, purpose)
examples: Context signals: content type, intended author, target audience, domain expertise
Detection triggers: explicit voice request, domain keywords, author attribution
Edge case handling: Justin writing about real estate vs Laura's MLS listings

lexicon.definition: Rules and triggers for transitioning between different voice modes during content generation
examples: Explicit switching: User requests specific voice or author change
Automatic switching: Domain change triggers voice recalibration
Priority hierarchy: Author voice > domain voice > default voice

lexicon.definition: Catalog of available voices with characteristics, use cases, and activation triggers
examples: LSH voice: romantic MLS style for Laura's real estate listings
WCB Professional: polished business communication
WCB Warm & Witty: approachable updates and conversational content
8825 Governance: concise technical structured format

lexicon.definition: Modular framework with 15 research techniques auto-selected based on research type, goal, and stakes
examples: Modules: charter.make, discover.topics, evidence.scan, questions.expand
history.map, dataset.analyze, gap.find, method.design, sources.audit
signals.predict, ethics.check, abstract.compress, hypothesis.generate
hybrid.invent, quiz.check - tiered automatic selection

lexicon.definition: Three learning depth levels with invisible assessment of complexity, foundation, and importance
examples: Fast: simplify, translate, check, refine (quick foundational learning)
Standard: + map, roleplay, plan, memory (comprehensive understanding)
Mastery: + multi-voice (deep expertise with multiple perspectives)
Auto-triggered based on inferred variables without user prompt

lexicon.definition: Founder-as-Systems-Architect voice positioning 8825 as persistent organizational AI partner
examples: Core claim: enables AI to remember, reason, and operate across time
Alignment: persistent memory thesis + multi-agent orchestration
Infrastructure play: sits above models for durable value
Strategic but warm tone for general investor narrative

lexicon.definition: UI interface pattern where widget floats in bottom-right corner of active window across all applications
examples: Always-on-top overlay that follows active window using macOS Accessibility API
Similar to time trackers (RescueTime), screen recorders (Loom), transcription tools (Scribe)
Requires user permission, polls window position every ~100ms for positioning
Use cases: persistent time tracking, quick actions, context-aware tools

technical_notes.constraints: Permission friction, CPU/battery impact, content obscuring

lexicon.status: Exploration phase - potential UI interface option for 8825

lexicon.definition: System for logging actual task duration and using patterns to predict future work timeframes
examples: Track active engagement time from initiation to final JSON integration
Build estimation model from historical patterns (simple analysis ~10-15min, complex ~30-45min)
Suggest highest-priority tasks that fit available time window with 10-20% buffer
Enable 'What can I do in 15 minutes?' workflow optimization

duration_categories.quick_wins: 5-15 minutes (simple edits, single-file updates, quick analysis)
duration_categories.standard_tasks: 15-30 minutes (multi-file changes, moderate research, pattern extraction)
duration_categories.complex_initiatives: 30-60+ minutes (new system design, cross-project integration, deep analysis)

lexicon.implementation: Add 'duration_estimate' and 'actual_duration' fields to summary_log entries

agent_references.note: Agent capabilities moved to projects/agent_registry.json to prevent duplication
agent_references.reconstruction: Use 'recreate WCB for <agent_id>' to regenerate from registry

Use three-beat storytelling pattern for cognitive chunking and retention
Leverage HTML + CSS for document generation with visual fidelity
Build on proven joju resume rendering patterns for general documents
Use Pandoc for multi-format conversion when document features needed
Start with PDF export, expand to other formats based on actual needs
HTML + CSS document generation workflow
Pandoc multi-format conversion integration
Template-based export system design
Physical-digital integration for tangible memory objects
Cross-brand collaboration with material craft partnerships
Kickstarter positioning for limited-run narrative products
Serialized identity linking physical objects to digital memory
Use Magic Lenses multi-perspective evaluation to avoid single-bias thinking
Prioritize reaction signals over polite feedback for validation
Run tiny validation loops until approach clicks naturally

Interview users to observe real workflow pain points
Make complex systems simple for user adoption
Focus on smallest unit of value that proves usefulness
Ship and learn rather than wait for perfect solution
Detect voice requirements from context before content generation
Switch voices explicitly when context changes (author, domain, audience)

checklists.name: RSS Automation Workflow Design
items: Use targeted search RSS URLs with embedded keywords (not generic feeds)
Filter at source, not after full feed pull
Implement multiple focused feeds over single broad feed
Parse XML with specific field extraction logic
Integrate LLM for profile generation and scoring
Connect to Notion/database for candidate storage

summary_log.topic: Beta tester recruitment via Reddit RSS analysis
summary_log.notes: Systematic beta tester identification workflow. Analyzed Reddit RSS feeds for keywords related to portfolio/profile tools. Developed comprehensive user profiling methodology with information asymmetry risk assessment.

Extracted Rule of Three pedagogical framework from design systems class creation session. Added patterns for educational content structuring (three-beat storytelling, cognitive chunking), vocabulary alternatives for technical accessibility, and governance documentation practices. Framework applicable beyond design systems to general technical education and content creation.

summary_log.topic: Document generation and export workflow analysis
summary_log.notes: Comprehensive analysis of document generation options: HTML+CSS pipeline (excellent for PDFs, leverages joju patterns, visual fidelity) vs Pandoc (universal converter, 30+ formats, document features like TOC/citations). Recommended hybrid approach: start with HTML+CSS for PDF export building on proven joju resume rendering, add Pandoc later for multi-format needs. Identified Windsurf workspace limitations and export destinations.

Extracted cross-project collaboration patterns from MTRL TrustyBits brainstorm: physical-digital integration concepts, serialized identity linking tangible objects to 8825 memory entries, Kickstarter positioning for narrative products, and microbatch storytelling approaches. Added lexicon for physical-digital twins and limited-run product strategies.

summary_log.topic: Chat Mining Agent strategic frameworks extraction
summary_log.notes: Extracted strategic validation frameworks from Chat Mining Agent WCB: Magic Lenses multi-perspective evaluation system, tiny loops validation methodology, Founding Hypothesis template, reaction-over-feedback prioritization, and integration discovery patterns. Added customer-first principles and decision-making frameworks for cross-project application.

summary_log.topic: Voice detection and switching framework formalization
summary_log.notes: Created formal voice management framework addressing detection, switching logic, and edge case handling. Established voice registry concept for cataloging available voices (LSH romantic MLS, WCB Professional/Warm, 8825 Governance). Defined context signals for voice detection (author, domain, audience, purpose) and switching triggers (explicit requests, domain changes, author attribution). Formalized edge case handling with priority hierarchy: author voice > domain voice > default voice.

summary_log.topic: Research intelligence layer and learning adaptive modes implementation
summary_log.notes: Added 15-module research intelligence framework with tiered auto-selection (Tier1/2/3) based on research type, goal, and stakes. Implemented 3-level learning adaptive modes (fast/standard/mastery) with invisible assessment engine for complexity, foundation, and strategic importance. Added Sequoia-specific positioning narrative with Founder-as-Systems-Architect voice positioning 8825 as persistent memory and multi-agent orchestration infrastructure play.

summary_log.topic: Window-hovering widget UI pattern feasibility analysis
summary_log.notes: Explored feasibility of always-on-top widget that nests in bottom-right corner of active window across all applications. Analyzed technical approach using macOS Accessibility API (CGWindowListCopyWindowInfo) for window tracking and floating overlay windows. Identified time tracking apps (RescueTime, Timing) and screen recorders (Loom, Scribe) as reference implementations. Verdict: Feasible with medium complexity (4-6 weeks MVP). Primary constraints are permission friction (Accessibility approval) and performance optimization. Alternative browser extension approach for web-only use cases. Added as potential UI interface option for 8825 context-aware tooling.

Added duration tracking system to enable pattern recognition and time-boxed task recommendations. Implemented 'duration_estimate' and 'actual_duration' fields in summary_log. Defined three task categories: quick wins (5-15min), standard tasks (15-30min), complex initiatives (30-60+min). Goal: Enable 'what's next in 15 minutes?' workflow optimization with 10-20% buffer built into estimates. System will learn from historical patterns to improve estimation accuracy over time.
